summary: Henry II was the eldest child of Empress Matilda and Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou, born at Le Mans on 5 March 1133. In May 1152 he added the duchy of Aquitaine to his domains by marrying its duchess, Eleanor, recently divorced from the French king, and on 19 December 1154 he was crowned king of England at Westminster. He died at Chinon on 6 July 1189.

citations.title: Dictionary of National Biography, 1885-1900/Henry II
citations.quote: Henry II (1133-1189), king of England, was the eldest child of Matilda, daughter of Henry I, and Geoffrey Plantagenet (1113-1150), count of Anjou.

summary: Richard I, called Richard Coeur-de-Lion, was the third son of Henry II and Eleanor of Aquitaine, born at Oxford on 8 September 1157. En route to the Third Crusade he married Berengaria of Navarre at Limassol, Cyprus, on 12 May 1191. He was killed by a crossbow bolt while besieging the castle of Chalus in 1199.

name: John

citations.quote: probably born at Oxford on 24 Dec. 1167

citations.title: John, King of England - Wikipedia
citations.quote: John was born in England around Christmastide in 1166 or 1167; the precise date is a subject of debate among historians.

citations.title: Dictionary of National Biography, 1885-1900/John (1167?-1216)
citations.quote: He died on the 19th

citations.title: John, King of England - Wikipedia
citations.quote: Died 19 October 1216 (aged 49) Newark Castle, Nottinghamshire, England

citations.title: Dictionary of National Biography, 1885-1900/John (1167?-1216)

citations.title: Isabella, Countess of Gloucester - Wikipedia

citations.title: Isabella of Angouleme - Wikipedia
citations.quote: Married on 24 August 1200, in Angouleme

summary: John, youngest son of Henry II and Eleanor of Aquitaine, was probably born at Oxford on 24 December 1167, though the precise date is debated. He married Isabella, Countess of Gloucester in 1189 (a union annulled around 1200) and then Isabella of Angouleme in 1200. He was crowned king at Westminster in 1199 and, faced with baronial rebellion, sealed Magna Carta at Runnymede on 15 June 1215; he died at Newark Castle on 19 October 1216.

citations.title: Dictionary of National Biography, 1885-1900/John (1167?-1216)
citations.quote: on the 15th [June 1215], when John met the barons at Runnymede...He was...set his seal to the Great Charter (Magna Carta).

citations.title: John, King of England - Wikipedia
citations.quote: John's mother was Eleanor, the powerful duchess of Aquitaine.

uncertain: Birth date disputed: DNB gives 'probably' 24 Dec. 1167 at Oxford, while modern sources hedge between Christmastide 1166 and 1167 and do not always confirm the birthplace. DNB's Henry III entry states John died 'on the 18th' of October 1216, whereas DNB's own John entry and Wikipedia give 19 October 1216; the marriage date/location of John and Isabella of Angouleme is also inconsistent between DNB ('probably' at Chinon, c. 30 July 1200) and modern sources (24 August 1200, Angouleme).

citations.quote: was married to the king at Canterbury by Archbishop Edmund on 14 Jan. 1236

summary: Henry III, elder son of King John and Isabella of Angouleme, was born at Winchester on 1 October 1207 and was crowned as a nine-year-old at Gloucester on 28 October 1216 amid civil war. He married Eleanor of Provence at Canterbury on 14 January 1236. Baronial opposition led by Simon de Montfort forced reforms and civil war in the 1260s, and Henry died at Westminster on 16 November 1272 after a reign of over fifty-six years, the longest in medieval English history.

citations.title: Dictionary of National Biography, 1885-1900/Henry III
citations.quote: elder son of John [q. v.] by his queen Isabella [q. v.] of Angouleme

name: Eleanor of Aquitaine

citations.title: Eleanor of Aquitaine - Wikipedia
citations.quote: the date of her birth was once given as 1122 or 1124, the latter is now generally accepted

citations.quote: annulled on 21 March [1152] on grounds of consanguinity within the fourth degree

citations.quote: 18 May 1152 at Poitiers Cathedral, a quiet private ceremony

summary: Eleanor, daughter of Duke William X of Aquitaine and Aenor de Chatellerault, was born in Bordeaux around 1124. Her marriage to Louis VII of France was annulled in March 1152 on grounds of consanguinity, and weeks later, on 18 May 1152, she married Henry, duke of Normandy and soon king of England. She died at Fontevraud Abbey on 1 April 1204.

citations.title: Eleanor of Aquitaine - Wikipedia
citations.quote: Duke William X of Aquitaine and his wife, Aenor de Chatellerault

uncertain: Birth year disputed among sources: earlier scholarship gave 1122, but 1124 is now generally accepted; the exact date is unknown.

name: William, Count of Poitiers

citations.title: William IX, Count of Poitiers - Wikipedia
citations.quote: Born 17 August 1153 in Normandy or Poitiers, Aquitaine

citations.title: William IX, Count of Poitiers - Wikipedia
citations.quote: William is reported to have died either at the age of 2 in April 1156, or at the age of 3 on 2 December 1156, after suffering a seizure at Wallingford Castle.

summary: William, Count of Poitiers, was the first son of King Henry II and Duchess Eleanor of Aquitaine, born on 17 August 1153. He died as a young child at Wallingford Castle and was buried at Reading Abbey at the feet of his great-grandfather Henry I.

citations.title: William IX, Count of Poitiers - Wikipedia
citations.quote: William, Count of Poitiers (17 August 1153 - 1156) was the first son of King Henry II of England and Duchess Eleanor of Aquitaine.

uncertain: Sources disagree on his exact death date: either April 1156 (aged 2) or 2 December 1156 (aged 3, after a seizure at Wallingford Castle).

name: Henry the Young King

titles.title: King of England (junior co-ruler, crowned during his father's lifetime)

citations.title: Henry the Young King - Wikipedia
citations.quote: In 1170, he became titular King of England

citations.title: Henry the Young King - Wikipedia
citations.quote: 28 February 1155 London, England

citations.title: Henry the Young King - Wikipedia
citations.quote: 11 June 1183 (aged 28) Castle of Martel, Lot, France

citations.title: Henry the Young King - Wikipedia
citations.quote: Young Henry and Margaret were formally married on 27 August 1172 at Winchester Cathedral

summary: Henry, the second son of Henry II and Eleanor of Aquitaine, was crowned junior King of England in 1170 during his father's lifetime, the only such coronation after the Norman Conquest, and is not counted in the regnal numbering. He married Margaret of France in 1172 and died in 1183 while in rebellion against his father.

citations.title: Henry the Young King - Wikipedia
citations.quote: the only English king since the Norman Conquest to be crowned during his father's reign

uncertain: Henry the Young King was crowned and titled King of England alongside his father but never reigned as sole monarch and predeceased Henry II; he is excluded from the standard regnal list, unlike his brothers Richard I and John.

citations.title: Matilda, Duchess of Saxony - Wikipedia
citations.quote: Henry the Lion met his bride in Minden, where they were married in the local Cathedral by Bishop Werner on 1 February 1168

summary: Matilda, third child and eldest daughter of Henry II and Eleanor of Aquitaine, was born in or around June 1156. She married Duke Henry the Lion of Saxony at Minden Cathedral on 1 February 1168 and died in Brunswick in 1189.

citations.title: Matilda, Duchess of Saxony - Wikipedia
citations.quote: third child and eldest daughter of King Henry II of England and Eleanor of Aquitaine

uncertain: Sources give five different dates for her death in 1189 (8, 20, 28 June, 3 or 13 July).

summary: Geoffrey, son of Henry II and Eleanor of Aquitaine, was born on 23 September 1158 and became Duke of Brittany through his marriage to Constance in July 1181. He died in Paris in August 1186, leaving two daughters and a posthumous son, Arthur.

summary: Eleanor, second daughter of Henry II of England and Eleanor of Aquitaine, was born at Domfront in Normandy around 1161. She married King Alfonso VIII of Castile at Burgos in 1170 and died there in 1214. She is distinct from her great-granddaughter Eleanor of Castile, who married Edward I.

citations.title: Eleanor of England, Queen of Castile - Wikipedia
citations.quote: the second daughter of King Henry II of England and his wife Eleanor, Duchess of Aquitaine

summary: Joan, seventh child of Henry II and Eleanor of Aquitaine, was born in October 1165. She married King William II of Sicily in 1177, and after his death married Raymond VI of Toulouse in 1196. She died in childbirth at Rouen in September 1199.

citations.title: Joan of England, Queen of Sicily - Wikipedia

summary: Berengaria, daughter of Sancho VI of Navarre and Sancha of Castile, was born around 1165-1170. She married Richard I of England at Limassol, Cyprus, on 12 May 1191; the marriage produced no children. She died at L'Epau Abbey near Le Mans in 1230.

citations.title: Berengaria of Navarre - Wikipedia
citations.quote: Whether the marriage was ever even consummated is a matter for conjecture.

uncertain: Her birth year is given only as a range, c. 1165-1170, by Wikipedia; no precise date is established.

name: Isabella, Countess of Gloucester

citations.title: Isabella, Countess of Gloucester - Wikipedia
citations.quote: Isabella died on 14 October 1217

citations.title: Isabella, Countess of Gloucester - Wikipedia
citations.quote: Isabella was removed from royal wardship and was married to John on 29 August at Marlborough Castle

summary: Isabella, daughter of William Fitz Robert, 2nd Earl of Gloucester, was born around 1160-1166. She married the future King John at Marlborough Castle on 29 August 1189; the marriage, which produced no children, was annulled on grounds of consanguinity around 1200. She died in 1217.

citations.title: Isabella, Countess of Gloucester - Wikipedia
citations.quote: William Fitz Robert, 2nd Earl of Gloucester

uncertain: Wikipedia gives only a range for her birth, c. 1160-1166. The Dictionary of National Biography calls her 'Avice of Gloucester' rather than Isabella.

name: Isabella of Angouleme

citations.title: Isabella of Angouleme - Wikipedia
citations.quote: 4 June 1246 Fontevraud Abbey, France

citations.title: Isabella of Angouleme - Wikipedia
citations.quote: Married on 24 August 1200, in Angouleme

summary: Isabella, daughter of Aymer, Count of Angouleme, and Alice of Courtenay, married King John in 1200 while still very young. Their five children all survived to adulthood, including the future Henry III. She died at Fontevraud Abbey in 1246.

citations.title: Isabella of Angouleme - Wikipedia
citations.quote: when she was "12 or 14" years old

uncertain: Sources disagree on her exact birth year (c. 1186 or c. 1188) and therefore on her age at marriage to John in 1200, variously given as 12 or 14.

name: Arthur I, Duke of Brittany

citations.title: Arthur I, Duke of Brittany - Wikipedia
citations.quote: 29 March 1187 Nantes, Brittany

citations.title: Arthur I, Duke of Brittany - Wikipedia
citations.quote: Disappeared 1203 (aged 15-16)

summary: Arthur, posthumous son of Geoffrey II, Duke of Brittany, was proclaimed heir presumptive to Richard I in 1190. On Richard's death in 1199 the throne passed instead to Arthur's uncle John; Arthur, who continued to press his claim with French support, was captured in 1202 and vanished in April 1203 while a prisoner of John.

citations.title: Arthur I, Duke of Brittany - Wikipedia
citations.quote: the king proclaimed his brother John as his heir, fearing Arthur was too young

uncertain: Arthur was a rival claimant to the English throne against his uncle John; he never reigned and the circumstances and exact date of his death (he 'vanished in April 1203') remain unknown, with contemporary chroniclers alleging John killed him personally.

name: Eleanor, Fair Maid of Brittany

citations.title: Eleanor, Fair Maid of Brittany - Wikipedia
citations.quote: Eleanor of Brittany (c. 1184 - 10 August 1241)

death.place: Bristol Castle or Corfe Castle, Dorset

citations.title: Eleanor, Fair Maid of Brittany - Wikipedia
citations.quote: 10 August 1241 (aged c. 57-59)

summary: Eleanor, daughter of Geoffrey II, Duke of Brittany, was held captive by her uncle King John from around 1199-1200 and remained imprisoned for the rest of her life on the orders of John and then Henry III, dying at Bristol or Corfe Castle in 1241 after decades of confinement.

citations.title: Eleanor, Fair Maid of Brittany - Wikipedia
citations.quote: Before his death John stated that Eleanor should never be released.

summary: Richard, second son of King John and Isabella of Angouleme, was born at Winchester Castle on 5 January 1209. Made Earl of Cornwall, he married three times and in 1257 was elected King of the Romans by German electoral princes. He died at Berkhamsted Castle in 1272.

summary: Isabella, fourth child and second daughter of King John and Isabella of Angouleme, was born around 1214. She married Holy Roman Emperor Frederick II at Worms in July 1235, becoming Holy Roman Empress, and died in childbirth at Foggia in December 1241.

summary: Eleanor, youngest child of King John and Isabella of Angouleme, was born in 1215 at Gloucester. Widowed by William Marshal, 2nd Earl of Pembroke, in 1231, she married Simon de Montfort, 6th Earl of Leicester, in a secret ceremony at Westminster in January 1238. She died at Montargis Abbey in France in 1275.

citations.title: Eleanor of England, Countess of Leicester - Wikipedia
citations.quote: the youngest child of John, King of England and Isabella of Angouleme

name: Eleanor of Provence

summary: Eleanor, daughter of Ramon Berenguer V, Count of Provence, and Beatrice of Savoy, was born at Aix-en-Provence around 1223. She married Henry III of England at Canterbury Cathedral on 14 January 1236, becoming the mother of Edward I among five children, and died at Amesbury in 1291.

citations.title: Eleanor of Provence - Wikipedia
citations.quote: she was "either 12 or 13 years old, while he was 28"

uncertain: Her exact birth year is not firmly established; Matthew Paris records her as 'already twelve' on arrival in England in late 1235/early 1236, and sources give her age at marriage as either 12 or 13.

summary: Edmund, son of Henry III and Eleanor of Provence, was born in London on 16 January 1245 and made Earl of Lancaster in 1267. He married Aveline de Forz in 1269 and, after her death, Blanche of Artois around the end of 1275. He died at Bayonne in 1296.

citations.title: Edmund Crouchback - Wikipedia
citations.quote: King Henry III of England

uncertain: The exact day of his first marriage to Aveline de Forz is given as either 8 or 9 April 1269; the second marriage to Blanche of Artois is dated only to between December 1275 and January 1276.

summary: Margaret, daughter of Henry III and Eleanor of Provence, was born at Windsor Castle on 29 September 1240. She married King Alexander III of Scotland at York Minster on Christmas Day 1251, aged eleven, and died at Cupar Castle in 1275.

summary: Katherine, daughter of Henry III and Eleanor of Provence, was born on 25 November 1253 and died aged three on 3 May 1257. The chronicler Matthew Paris described her as unable to speak, and historians have long debated what disability or illness she may have had.

citations.title: Katherine of England - Wikipedia
